name: Missing Encryption of Sensitive Data
description: The product does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.

mapping.rationale: CWE-311 is high-level with more precise children available. It is a level-1 Class (i.e., a child of a Pillar).

mechanism.root_cause_summary: The product does not encrypt sensitive or critical information before storage or transmission.
typical_preconditions: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
typical_sources: untrusted or insufficiently validated state reaches sensitive operation
negative_nonfix_signals: style refactor without changing the sensitive path

guard_patterns: same logical object and same sink context

recommended_guard_templates: same logical object and same sink context

stage1.goal: Classify patch chunks by how directly they repair the CWE root cause.
must_focus: direct change to vulnerable sink or validation site
changes that alter boundary, lifecycle, policy, or authorization relation
supporting helper or error-propagation logic needed by the main fix
must_avoid: generic logging changes without security semantics
refactors unrelated to the sensitive sink/path

stage2.goal: Construct discriminative root cause, anchor, predicates, and guards.
must_focus: anchor at the sink or dominant validation block
prefer predicates that encode relations, not lone generic tokens
choose function-local or anchor-window scope whenever possible
must_avoid: predicates that rely on generic names alone
anchors in wrappers that lack the core vulnerability mechanism
recommended_queries: same logical object and same sink context

stage3.goal: Verify whether the target tag still exhibits the vulnerable relation or already contains the fix relation.
must_focus: whether the sensitive sink/path still exists
whether the fix relation is locally present in the same code region
whether anchor relocation is required before concluding absence
must_avoid: declaring NOT_AFFECTED from generic token absence alone
using broad token matches as final evidence

bad_examples.summary: This code writes a user's login information to a cookie so the user does not have to login again later.
The following code attempts to establish a connection, read in a password, then store it to a buffer.
bad_examples.snippet: server.sin_family = AF_INET; hp = gethostbyname(argv[1]); if (hp==NULL) error("Unknown host"); memcpy( (char *)&server.sin_addr,(char *)hp->h_addr,hp->h_length); if (argc < 3) port = 80; else port = (unsigned...

bad_examples.summary: The following code attempts to establish a connection to a site to communicate sensitive information.
bad_examples.snippet: try { URL u = new URL("http://www.secret.example.org/"); HttpURLConnection hu = (HttpURLConnection) u.openConnection(); hu.setRequestMethod("PUT"); hu.connect(); OutputStream os = hu.getOutputStream(); hu.disconnect();...

observed_examples.description: wireless battery product stores credentials and Personal Health Information (PHI) without encryption
password and username stored in cleartext in a cookie
password stored in cleartext in a file with insecure permissions
chat program disables SSL in some circumstances even when the user says to use SSL.
Chain: product uses an incorrect public exponent when generating an RSA key, which effectively disables the encryption
storage of unencrypted passwords in a database
